English:
This Mens Mental Health Awareness Month, lets break the silence. Society often tells men to "be strong," but real strength lies in acknowledging your struggles and reaching out.

1. Speaking up isnt weaknessits courage.
2. Your feelings are valid.
3.You dont have to face it alone.

Whether its stress, anxiety, depression, or just feeling overwhelmed your mental health matters. Talk to a friend, a counselor, or a support group. Asking for help is a sign of strength, not failure.
 You are human. You deserve support. You are not alone.
Kiswahili:
Mwezi huu wa Uhamasishaji wa Afya ya Akili ya Wanaume, hebu tuvunje ukimya. Mara nyingi jamii huwaambia wanaume "kuwa imara," lakini nguvu halisi iko katika kutambua mapambano yako na kufikia lengo.

1. Kuzungumza si udhaifu - ni ujasiri.

2. Hisia zako ni halali.

3. Huna haja ya kukabiliana nayo peke yako.

Iwe ni msongo wa mawazo, wasiwasi, mfadhaiko, au kuhisi tu kuzidiwa - afya yako ya akili ni muhimu. Zungumza na rafiki, mshauri, au kikundi cha usaidizi. Kuomba msaada ni ishara ya nguvu, si kushindwa.

Wewe ni binadamu. Unastahili usaidizi. Hauko peke yako.
